skill.name: conductor-new-track
skill.description: Create structured Conductor work tracks with specifications, implementation plans, metadata, and project index updates.
skill.summary: Plan new Conductor tracks from user input and create the supporting project documents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analysis results are largely false positives caused by Markdown backticks, templates, and relative links in SKILL.md. The skill does intentionally read and write Conductor project files, so publication is reasonable with a filesystem capability warning.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Writes Are Core Behavior
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to create track folders and write spec.md, plan.md, metadata.json, index.md, conductor/tracks.md, and conductor/index.md. This is expected for the workflow, but users should review generated files before continuing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited instructions explicitly create and update local project files. The behavior is scoped to Conductor documentation and appears legitimate.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md, but the cited content is Markdown inline code or fenced examples. No shell execution primitive, script, or command runner is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows backticks are used for Markdown formatting and templates. I found no executable Ruby, shell command substitution, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Detections Are Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The detected ../ sequences appear inside generated Markdown links back to Conductor project documents. They are not used as unsanitized file paths or instructions to access arbitrary parent directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are link targets in an index.md template. The surrounding context does not request traversal-based reads, writes, or secret access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Unsupported
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are prose, headings, or Markdown templates. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher use, or cryptographic operation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context around each static location and found planning text, not crypto code. The skill has no code dependency that performs hashing or encryption.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Detection Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance flag points to the Progress section of a generated index template. No networking commands, endpoints, port scanning, or host discovery instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is normal documentation text. Manual review found no network access or reconnaissance workflow in the skill.

content.user_title: Create Conductor Tracks With Clear Plans
content.value_statement: New work can lose context when requirements and tasks are scattered. This skill gathers inputs and creates Conductor track documents with specs, phases, and review steps.
actual_capabilities: Checks for required Conductor project files before creating a track.
Classifies work as feature, bug, chore, or refactor.
Guides users through focused specification questions.
Generates a track ID from the work summary and current date.
Creates specification, plan, metadata, and track index content.
Updates Conductor track lists and active track references.
limitations: Requires an existing Conductor setup with product, tech stack, and workflow files.
Depends on user answers to define accurate scope and acceptance criteria.
Does not implement the track after planning is complete.
May need manual edits when project conventions differ from the template.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a feature idea into a Conductor specification with user story, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and phased implementation tasks.

use_cases.title: Structure a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Capture reproduction steps, expected behavior, affected areas, and a focused fix plan before changing code.

use_cases.title: Prepare Maintenance Work
use_cases.description: Define chore or refactor work with motivation, success criteria, risk assessment, and clear verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Feature Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Conductor feature track for adding saved searches to the dashboard.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Bug Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a known defect
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bug track for reports that fail when the date range includes a leap day.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor track to split the billing service into smaller modules without changing behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Shape a Risky Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt with constraints and dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a feature track for migrating login to passkeys. Include rollout risks, dependencies, tests, and migration boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a feature track for CSV import in the admin console.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks focused questions, drafts the specification, proposes phases, and prepares track files after approval.

output_examples.input: Create a bug track for failed password reset emails.
output_examples.output: The assistant captures reproduction details, expected behavior, affected systems, and a phased verification plan.

output_examples.input: Create a chore track for dependency upgrades.
output_examples.output: The assistant records the motivation, success criteria, risks, and update plan for the Conductor track.

best_practices: Review generated specs before accepting the implementation plan.
Keep acceptance criteria specific, testable, and limited to the track scope.
Use the generated phases as review checkpoints during implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use one track for unrelated features or bug fixes.
Do not skip dependency and risk questions for cross-system changes.
Do not treat generated plans as final without project owner review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Conductor track with a specification, implementation plan, metadata, index, and registry updates.

faq.question: Does it require Conductor setup first?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects conductor/product.md, conductor/tech-stack.md, and conductor/workflow.md to exist.

faq.question: Can it plan bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. It has separate questions for reproduction steps, expected behavior, affected areas, and root cause hypotheses.

faq.question: Does it run implementation commands?
faq.answer: No. It creates planning documents and tells the user how to start implementation later.

faq.question: Why is there a filesystem risk factor?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to write Conductor planning files and update project indexes.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex.
